mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.94% as of 2007-09-01 reflects tightening cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1331.0 thousand units as of 2007-08-01 reflects housing market stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production over 3 months signals weakening productive capacity utilization and forward profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market weakness that reduces household income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices earnings risk.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite negative payroll changes, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation measured by CPI combined with elevated federal funds rates signals monetary tightening that constrains real consumer purchasing power and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 207.738 and FEDFUNDS at 4.94% as of 2007-09-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly signals underlying economic momentum; sustained quarterly growth above 2% produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings expectations remain elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11520.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2007-04-01
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during positive GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% combined with declining housing starts signals monetary tightening that constrains credit availability and housing demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as financial sector earnings deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.94% and HOUST at 1331.0 thousand as of 2007-09-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing market stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
